metadata.title:
MANY CITIES ARE


FIGHTING FOR IT

pages.raw_text:
CHICAGO. H., Dec. l6.-Pursuant
to the call of Chairman Oliver w.
Stewart the members of the prohibit
ton national committee went into ses-
sion at the Auditorium hotel this morn
ing for the purpose OF deciding upon
the date and place for holding the par
TVS national convention next year. The
national convention of the prohibition
lists always draws large crowd and %
a consequence the convention is sought
by many cities. 1t is an open fight
this year with more than half a dozen
cities among the contestants. Chica-
20, of course, is regarded as a strong
competitor, and st. Louis is another
city that is frequently mentioned as a
likely meeting place for the prohibl-
tionists. There appears, however, to
be strong sentiment among the COM
mitteemen in favor of holding the con-
vention in some smaller city. The
supporters of this idea argue that II
the prohibitionists meet in the same
city as either the republicans or DEM
ocrats they will be obliged to take
second place and will not receive as
much attention as they would if the
convention were held elsewhere. There
are a number of smaller and well locat-
ed cities bidding for the convention and
if the sentiment against Chicago and
st. Louis prevails fine field for the
